failure_surface: All test cases failed (test_case_results: [0]) despite formulas appearing syntactically correct. The rolling average formulas were generated but produced incorrect or unvalidated results.
root_cause: The agent wrote formulas without first validating that the source cell range contains appropriate data. Specifically, B12's formula (=AVERAGE(B$11:B12)) includes the target cell itself in its own calculation range, creating a circular/self-referential issue. Additionally, the agent did not inspect the actual contents of B11 to verify it contains a value rather than a formula or empty cell before generating dependent formulas.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before generating rolling average formulas, inspect the actual values in the source data range to ensure formulas do not create circular references or reference non-numeric/empty cells that would break the calculation chain.
evidence: Formula created: '=AVERAGE(B$11:B12)' in B12 - this includes B12 itself in the average calculation
No inspection of B11's contents before using it as the fixed start reference
Spreadsheet content shows 'NaN' values in Average column initially, indicating no prior data foundation
All test_case_results are 0, indicating complete formula/output validation failure

memory_items.title: Validate Source Data Before Formula Generation
memory_items.lesson: When creating formulas that depend on other cells, always read and validate the source cells' contents before writing formulas to avoid circular references or invalid calculations.
memory_items.generalization: Always inspect source range values programmatically before implementing dependent formulas to ensure proper data foundation exists.

memory_items.title: Avoid Self-Referencing Formulas in Rolling Calculations
memory_items.lesson: In rolling/cumulative calculations, ensure the formula in cell N does not include cell N in its own calculation range unless explicitly required by the task.
memory_items.generalization: For incremental calculations extending downward, the formula range typically starts above or at the first data row and excludes the formula's own cell.

memory_items.title: Verify Number Formatting Persists After Save
memory_items.lesson: Format changes applied via cell.number_format may not persist correctly after workbook save; verify final output formatting matches requirements.
memory_items.generalization: After applying cell formats, open the saved file programmatically to confirm formatting was preserved before considering the task complete.

edits.content:
## 6. Formula Safety & Validation
- **Pre-Validation**: Before writing formulas that depend on other cells, inspect the source cells' actual contents to ensure they contain valid numeric data.
- **Circular Reference Avoidance**: Verify formula ranges do not include the target cell itself unless explicitly required (e.g., B12 should not average B11:B12 if B12 is the formula destination).
- **Formatting Verification**: Confirm number formats persist after save by reading back formatted cells in the saved file.
